Comment rules: If a property or enum (e.g., EmptyParagraphExportMode) is unavailable, either upgrade the library or remove/comment the line.
Loading rules: Add the missing using directive for Aspose.Words.Loading to access LoadOptions.
Load the document via the Document constructor which auto‑detects the input format.
Use generic LoadOptions for auto‑detecting the input format instead of MarkdownLoadOptions.
Saving/Export rules: Use Document.Save(stream, SaveFormat) overload for in‑memory conversion.
Keep the SaveFormat set to Docm when constructing OoxmlSaveOptions.
Save using SaveFormat.Doc to produce a Word 97‑2003 .doc file.
Save each extracted page with a distinct file name.
Create ImageSaveOptions inside the loop to avoid reusing the same instance across pages.
When a specific SaveOptions class is used, you do not need to set SaveFormat manually; the class defines it.
Use the concrete PdfSaveOptions class from Aspose.Words.Saving instead of the non‑existent SaveOptions factory.
Add a using directive for Aspose.Words.Saving to make PdfSaveOptions available.
Instantiate PdfSaveOptions with `new PdfSaveOptions()` and set desired properties (e.g., UseHighQualityRendering).
Pass the PdfSaveOptions instance to Document.Save overload that accepts a SaveOptions parameter.
Use object initializer syntax for ImageSaveOptions to keep the code concise.
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Create the images folder when ExportImagesAsBase64 is false to avoid runtime errors.
